revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白山神社 (文京区)]][[zh:白山神社 (文京區)]]


{{Infobox religious building
|name   = Hakusan Shrine
|image   = File:Hakusanjinja-bunkyoku-main-a-June13-2015.jpg
|location = 5-31-26 Hakusan, Bunkyō-ku, Tokyo{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}
|ISO = JP-13
|latitude = 35.722083
|longitude = 139.750528
|deity   = {{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}}; {{ill|Kikurihime|qid=Q11619891|1=Kikurihime}}
|Rank   = Former {{ill|Jun-Chokusai-sha|ja|准勅祭社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun-Chokusai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東京十社|1=Tokyo Ten Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q164895}}, Former Gō-sha{{sfn|Koishikawa Ward Office|1935}}
|established = Tenryaku 2 (948)
|honden   = Nagare-zukuri
|festival   = September 21{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}
|map= Tokyo city
|religious_affiliation= {{ill|Hakusan cult|qid=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}
|prefecture= {{ill|Hakusan|qid=Q11579538|1=Hakusan}}
|coordinates= {{Coord|35.72208333|N|139.75052778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|part_of= {{ill|Tokyo-jissha|qid=Q164895|1=Tokyo Ten Shrines}}
|architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
}}
'''Hakusan Shrine''' (白山神社, Hakusan-jinja), previously known as '''Hakusan Gongen Shrine''', is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Hakusan|ja|白山 (文京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan|1=Hakusan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579538}}, Bunkyō-ku, Tokyo. It is the origin of the local area's name, "Hakusan."

== Enshrined Deities ==
Hakusan Shrine enshrines {{ill|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|ja|菊理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kikurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619891}}, {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q813858}}, and {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}.

== History ==
Established in Tenryaku 2 (948) by transferring the deity from {{ill|Shirayama Hime Shrine|en|Shirayama Hime Shrine|qid=Q704702|lt=Shirayama Hime Shrine|1=Shirayama Hime Shrine}} in Kaga Province to Hongo Motomachi in Toshima-gun, Musashi Province (currently Hongo 1-chome, Bunkyō-ku). During the Genna era (1615-1624), the shrine was relocated to Sugamohara (within the present-day {{ill|Koishikawa Botanical Garden|en|Koishikawa Botanical Garden|qid=Q1064892|lt=Koishikawa Botanical Gardens|1=Koishikawa Botanical Garden}}) by the order of Shogun Tokugawa Hidetada. It was moved again to its present site in Meireki 1 (1655) due to the construction of a residence for Tokugawa Tsunayoshi, the future fifth shogun, who revered the shrine along with his mother, Keishō-in. The shrine received continual patronage from the Tokugawa family and was designated a Jun-Chokusai-sha in the early Meiji era.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}

== Bunkyō Hydrangea Festival ==
In mid-June every year, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Bunkyō Hydrangea Festival|ja|文京あじさいまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyō Hydrangea Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504616|1=Bunkyō Hydrangea Festival}}, one of Bunkyō's Five Major Flower Festivals. Approximately 3,000 hydrangea plants bloom within the shrine grounds and neighboring {{ill|Hakusan Park (Bunkyō-ku)|ja|白山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Park (Bunkyō-ku)|1=Hakusan Park (Bunkyō-ku)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16283848}}.<ref>[http://www.city.bunkyo.lg.jp/sosiki_busyo_kouen_annai_kuritukouen_kouen_hakusan.html Hakusan Park (Bunkyō City website)]</ref> During the festival, a {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|qid=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka|1=Fujizuka}} (Mt. Fuji mound) is open to the public, and various events, including toothbrush memorial services and fairs, take place primarily on weekends.

== Subsidiary Shrines ==
* Kantō Matsuo Shrine
* Inari Shrine
* Sengen Shrine

== Access ==
* 2-minute walk from Exit A3 of {{ill|白山駅|en|白山駅|qid=Q842997|lt=Hakusan Station (Tokyo)|1=白山駅}} on the Toei Mita Line ({{OSM経路図|start=35.72179,139.75218|goal=35.72184,139.75058|name=Route Map}}).

<gallery>
Hakusan-jinja 2019a.jpg|Torii gate (June 19, 2019)
Hakusan-jinja-2.jpg|Pathway (June 9, 2009)
Hakusan-jinja 2019b.jpg|View towards the Main Hall (June 19, 2019)
Hakusan-jinja (Bunkyo) 2019.jpg|Main Hall (December 15, 2019)
Hakusan-jinja-5.jpg|Bunkyō Hydrangea Festival (June 9, 2009)
Hakusan-jinja-6.jpg|Same as left (June 9, 2009)
</gallery>
